title: T-Mobile Has Plenty to Gain From a $260 Billion German Merger
summary: You want European champions? Try this for size. Deutsche Telekom AG is considering taking full control of T-Mobile US Inc. in a move that could create a $260 billion transatlantic telecoms giant. It’s easy to see why the German suitor wants to go from being T-Mobile’s lead shareholder to its outright owner. The benefits for T-Mobile are less obvious — until you consider its future without a deal.

title: Zero: London Mayor Khan on 10 Years of Wins and Loses (Podcast)
summary: Sadiq Khan is about to complete 10 years as London’s mayor. He’s seen the city affected by Brexit, the pandemic and two energy crises. That hasn’t deterred him from deploying some of the strongest air-quality regulations in any city. But he hasn’t yet succeeded in getting enough homes built and struggled to keep improving the train network. title: Wall Street Group Urges SEC to Lift Private Asset Trading Ban
summary: Lobbyists for some of Wall Street’s biggest alternative asset managers are asking regulators to relax rules preventing the buying and selling of assets between funds overseen by the same company, a move they say will boost the adoption of private assets in retirement accounts.

title: ESG Currents: Debating Whether Climate Risk Is Already Priced In
summary: Two experts, one question: Is climate risk already priced in? On this week’s episode of the ESG Currents podcast, Bloomberg Intelligence EMEA ESG integration analyst Grace Osborne hosts an Oxford-style debate. Dr. Jakob Thomae, co-founder and CEO of Theia Finance Labs, argues for the motion, while Dr. Ben Caldecott, founding director of the Oxford Sustainable Finance Group and a coordinating lead author on finance for the IPCC’s Seventh Assessment Report, argues against it. title: Bessent Says US Allies in Gulf, Asia Requested Swap Lines
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday that many Persian Gulf allies along with a number of Asian nations have requested foreign exchange swap lines with the US, and pointed to the potential for such arrangements to support dollar-denominated lending overseas.

title: Boeing Narrows Cash Burn in First Quarter as Deliveries Rise
summary: Boeing Co. reported lower-than-expected cash outflow as it delivered the most aircraft in the first quarter since 2019, continuing its recovery with higher output and more steady operations at its defense and services units.

title: GE Vernova Surges After Quarterly Orders Topped All of Last Year
summary: GE Vernova Inc., one of the world’s only makers of big natural-gas turbines, soared the most in intraday trading since mid-December after its electrification unit sold more grid equipment, including substations and transformers, to data center customers in the first quarter than all of last year.

title: Oil Rises on Elusive Peace Deal Outlook, Iran Gunboat Attacks
summary: Oil inched higher as attempts to resolve the seven-week conflict between the US and Iran struggled, and Iranian gunboats opened fire on vessels near the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway.

title: Oil Servicer Weatherford Sees War Impacts Deepening This Quarter
summary: Weatherford International Plc., one of the world’s largest oilfield-service providers, is warning that a hit to earnings in the wake of the Iran war will get worse this quarter, before an eventual rebound.

title: Emerging Currencies Waver as Conflict Drags On, Oil Prices Rise
summary: Emerging-market currencies faltered Wednesday as money managers waded through the latest raft of headlines out of the Middle East, weighing President Donald Trump’s truce extension against Iran’s attacks on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: S&P 500 Hovers Near Its All-Time High; Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
summary: A slew of strong corporate profits lifted stocks a day after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, though attempts to resolve the conflict struggled without any signs that the key Strait of Hormuz will reopen soon. Oil climbed.

title: United CEO Sidesteps Merger Topic, Says Assets of Interest
summary: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby sidestepped questions about a potential merger with American Airlines Group Inc. while acknowledging that the current climate of high costs and struggling rivals might offer opportunities for deals.

title: S&P Global Vice Chairman: Hormuz Crisis Is Driving Global Change
summary:
Consumers are already feeling the effects of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz with energy prices surging around the world. But what if the crisis is signaling a longer-term global power shift?

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway that a "different world" will emerge, regardless of the war's ultimate outcome. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blackstone Credit Fund Looks to Raise at Least $500 Million
summary: Blackstone Private Credit Fund is seeking to raise at least $500 million from an investment-grade note sale, people with direct knowledge of the matter said, as private lenders known as business development companies ramp up debt issuance after a dry spell.

title: Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Hole Will Linger Long After War
summary: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal. Speaking at the FT Commodities Global Summit in Lausanne, executives at some of the world’s largest oil traders warned that the rewiring of the oil market would take months even if a peace deal is agreed soon. The market is not fully reflecting the impact of the massive supply disruption, they said, cautioning that prices will need to ratchet higher to the point of pushing the global economy toward a recession if the conflict continues. Samantha Dart, Goldman Sachs Co-Head of Global Commodities Research, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. She speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Gain as Trump Truce Extension, Earnings Lift Spirits
summary: US stocks advanced on Wednesday as traders examined the state of play for the Middle East following President Donald Trump’s ceasefire extension with Iran and cheered corporate earnings.

title: Gold Recovers After Two-Day Drop as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire
summary: Gold regained some ground after a two-day decline, as US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and allowed more time to arrange fresh peace talks.

title: TD Bank Mulls Hedging Data Center Debt With Rare SRT Deal
summary: Toronto-Dominion Bank is weighing a rare type of significant risk transfer to hedge current and future data center exposure as tech companies ramp up artificial intelligence investment.

title: BRB Shareholders Greenlight Capital Raise of Up to $1.8 Billion
summary: Banco de Brasilia SA’s shareholders approved a capital raise of as much as 8.8 billion reais ($1.8 billion) to help bolster the Brazilian bank after it was stung by transactions it made with the failed Banco Master SA.

title: Toronto Exchange Owner to Buy Cboe’s Australia, Canada Units
summary: Toronto Stock Exchange owner TMX Group Ltd. is set to acquire Cboe Global Markets Inc.’s Australian and Canadian units.

title: A Private Equity Reset
summary: Private equity is entering a new era according to Mark Sotir, the President of  Equity Group Investments, He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why founders are rushing for stability, how AI is boosting productivity, not replacing jobs, and why rising interest rates and competition are making it harder than ever to generate returns. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UBS Faces $20 Billion Hike in Capital Needs Despite Concessions
summary: The Swiss government outlined plans that would add $20 billion to UBS Group AG’s capital needs, even as it waters down some planned changes to regulations set to hit the nation’s largest bank.

title: Avis Surge Crushes Short Sellers
summary: Avis shares tripled in two weeks, crushing short sellers with $2.5 billion in losses. But bears are doubling down—short interest now near a decade high at 58%. Analysts question the rally’s fundamentals, warning the surge looks like a short squeeze, not real growth. Bloomberg's Jordan Fitzgerald joined Bloomberg Open Interest with details. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hormuz Traffic at Near-Halt as Ships Come Under Gunfire
summary: The Strait of Hormuz stayed at a near standstill Wednesday as Iranian forces opened fire on — and seized — commercial ships.

title: Petrobras Board Member Seeks Fuel Price Increase to Stem Losses
summary: A Petrobras board member appointed by non-controlling shareholders is warning that the state-controlled producer is losing billions from below-market fuel sales and risks reputational damage.

title: Chip Stocks Rally for Record 16th Straight Day on AI Optimism
summary: An index tracking semiconductor stocks is on track for its all-time longest streak of daily gains, as investors anticipate strong growth for the sector driven by demand related to artificial intelligence.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 4/22/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Surging Fuel Prices Provide Warning Sign for UK Inflation
Markets Vulnerable But Buoyed by Economy, Seema Shah Says
summary: Seema Shah, Principal Asset Management chief global strategist, discusses the outlook for US equities amid recent geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices. She speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: United CEO Eyes Assets, But Won't Say From Which Airline
summary: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby sidestepped a question about if he's interested in buying rival American Airlines. But he did say discussions have been held with one airline about buying some assets. He also spoke to Lisa Abramowicz in Chicago about jet fuel prices, earnings and travel demand. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Commodity Traders Reap a Profit Bonanza from War Oil Shock
summary: The world’s biggest energy traders are reaping a fresh profit bonanza as the war in Iran causes unprecedented disruption to oil markets.

title: UK Electricity From Fossil Fuels Drops to Record Low of Just 2%
summary: UK power generation from fossil fuels fell to the lowest level since records started in 2009, highlighting the country’s rapid shift toward renewable energy.

title: Junk Bond Investors Are Squeezing Companies for Better Terms
summary: Investors are seizing what may be a fleeting opportunity to negotiate better terms when buying high-yield debt, turning the tables on borrowers for the first time in years.
